cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Jennifer could have gone to the swimming pool. The question gives several time periods when she was seen doing different activities. I need to look for gaps in her schedule where she wasn't doing any of those things, and also make sure the swimming pool was still open. First, let's list out all the times she was with other people: 1. Jennifer woke up at 5am. 2. Richard saw her working at the office from 5am to 8am. 3. Samantha saw her at the library reading from 12pm to 1pm. 4. Betty saw her buying a bike at the bike shop from 1pm to 2pm. 5. Sarah saw her buying a phone from 2pm to 9pm. 6. Thomas saw her walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. 7. The swimming pool was closed after 10pm. Now let's see what's in between these times. From 5am to 8am: working at the office. So she can't go to the pool then. From 8am to 12pm: What's here? The next thing is at 12pm she's at the library. So between 8am and 12pm, there's a gap. Is there anything else? No, Richard only saw her from 5am to 8am, and Samantha starts at 12pm. So between 8am and 12pm, she's unaccounted for. From 12pm to 1pm: at the library. Then from 1pm to 2pm: buying a bike. From 2pm to 9pm: buying a phone. From 9pm to 10pm: walking to the Statue of Liberty. The swimming pool was closed after 10pm, so she had to go before that. So the only unaccounted time is from 8am to 12pm. That would be the only window where she could have gone to the swimming pool. Let me check the options: Option A: 8am to 12pm.
